source_pr.title: expression, types: freeze unsafe datetime warning args

symptoms: Streamhelper advancer tests were flaky because they asserted immediately after unregister/pause without waiting for advancer state (`HasTask`) to reflect task removal, and because they used `assert.NoError` inside `require.Eventually`, which can permanently mark the test failed on transient errors.
failure_signature: Eventually-based assertions fail intermittently due to missing waits for task removal and using `assert.*` inside `require.Eventually` (early transient failures poison the test).
root_cause_explanation: These advancer unit tests were timing-sensitive: after unregistering a task, the advancer may not immediately reflect `HasTask()==false`, so assertions can race the state transition. Separately, using `assert.NoError(t, ...)` inside `require.Eventually` can call `t.Errorf()` on transient failures and permanently fail the test even if later retries would succeed. The PR adds explicit `Eventually` waits for task removal and switches to `EventuallyWithT` / safe checks so retries don't poison the test.
fix_pattern: Wait for advancer task removal with `require.Eventually` (e.g., `!adv.HasTask()`) and use `require.EventuallyWithT` for assertions inside Eventually loops to avoid hard-failing on transient errors.

evidence: evidence: add `Eventually` waits for `adv.HasTask()` after unregister and replace `Eventually`+`assert.*` with `EventuallyWithT` to avoid poisoned retries.

analysis:
PR title: expression, types: freeze unsafe datetime warning args

+ require.EventuallyWithT(... assert.NoError(c, adv.OnTick(ctx)) ...)
